Title: UV-B increases active phytochrome B to suppress thermomorphogenesis and enhance UV-B stress tolerance at high temperatures

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes UV-B-induced flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis and enhances UV-B stress tolerance at high temperature; also suppresses thermomorphogenic growth
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein present in seedlings; active photobody number reduced at high temperature and increased by UV-B
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (phyB-9); overexpression (PHYB-OX; 35S:phyB-GFP); immunoblot; photobody imaging; qRT-PCR of CHI/CHS/FLS1/PHR1; anthocyanin measurement; UV-B survival assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for phyB-GFP; overexpression line PHYB-OX
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature (28°C); narrowband UV-B pretreatment; damaging broadband UV-B challenge
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phyB accumulates to higher levels and forms more photobodies when COP1 is inactive or when plants are exposed to UV-B. 2. phyB suppresses PIF4 abundance/activity and thermomorphogenesis. 3. phyB is required for full UV-B induction of flavonoid-biosynthetic genes, anthocyanin accumulation, and UV-B acclimation/tolerance at high temperature.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher phyB activity suppresses thermomorphogenic hypocotyl elongation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increasing active phyB could improve UV-B tolerance in warm environments while restraining excessive thermomorphogenic growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because it is a photoreceptor/signaling component rather than a metabolic enzyme or TF; source and applied species are the same in this study.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of UV-B-induced flavonoid biosynthetic protection indirectly by destabilizing phyB and HFR1; its inhibition by UVR8 promotes anthocyanin-associated UV-B tolerance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally assayed in seedlings under white light, high temperature, and UV-B
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Analysis of cop1-4 and cop1-6 mutants; immunoblot of phyB, PIF4, CRY1; photobody imaging; hypocotyl assays; qRT-PCR of CHI and CHS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COP1 activity is required for thermomorphogenesis and for low active-phyB/HFR1 states. 2. UV-B acting through UVR8 inhibits COP1, allowing phyB and HFR1 accumulation. 3. cop1 mutants are defective in UV-B-responsive HY5 and flavonoid-biosynthetic gene induction, indicating COP1 is central to the UV-B signaling module affecting protective metabolism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of COP1 strongly impairs thermomorphogenic growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Transient or context-specific inhibition of COP1 signaling could enhance UV-B protective metabolism, but constitutive disruption would severely alter development
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is a primary experimentally tested signaling node controlling metabolite-related UV-B acclimation, though not itself a metabolic enzyme.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with phyB to suppress thermomorphogenesis under UV-B, thereby supporting UV-B tolerance indirectly
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein level increased by UV-B at both 20°C and 28°C in seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hfr1-201 mutant; cop1-6;phyB-CR;hfr1-201 triple mutant; HFR1-GFP immunoblot; hypocotyl assays; qRT-PCR of YUC8 and IAA19; UV-B survival assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: HFR1-GFP transgenic line promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HFR1 accumulates when COP1 is inactive and is stabilized by UV-B. 2. HFR1 acts redundantly with phyB to repress PIF4-mediated thermomorphogenesis under UV-B. 3. Loss of HFR1 together with phyB reduces UV-B tolerance more than loss of phyB alone, linking growth suppression to stress acclimation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppresses growth responses at elevated temperature
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating HFR1 together with phyB may tune the balance between growth and UV-B protection under warm conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as Common_Gene because it is a non-DNA-binding HLH modulator rather than a canonical TF or enzyme.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly with phyB and HFR1 to suppress thermomorphogenic growth and thereby supports UV-B tolerance indirectly
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein elevated in cop1 mutants at both 20°C and 28°C; not significantly induced by UV-B
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR cry1-CR in cbh background; immunoblot; hypocotyl assays; qRT-PCR of PIF4 targets; UV-B response assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRY1 accumulates in cop1 mutants and further suppresses PIF4-dependent thermomorphogenesis. 2. cry1 mutation enhances thermoresponsive growth in cbh mutants and modestly contributes under UV-B. 3. Its contribution to UV-B protective metabolism is indirect through growth repression rather than direct flavonoid induction.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased CRY1 activity restrains elongation growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be combined with phyB/HFR1 pathway engineering to optimize stress-protective growth restraint
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as Common_Gene because it is a photoreceptor/signaling factor, not a metabolic enzyme or transcription factor.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone, initiating flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies committed precursor for anthocyanin/flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UV-B-induced CHS expression is reduced in phyB mutants, linking the gene to phyB-dependent flavonoid accumulation and UV-B tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: UV-B inducible in seedlings at 28°C; induction depends partly on phyB and COP1 pathway status
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in wild type and phyB mutants; anthocyanin assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native gene; no promoter manipulation reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS is induced by UV-B at high temperature. 2. This induction is attenuated in phyB-9 mutants. 3. Reduced CHS expression correlates with decreased anthocyanin accumulation and lower UV-B tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for boosting UV-B-protective flavonoid accumulation, potentially in combination with phyB activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as a downstream metabolic readout rather than a direct focal mechanistic gene, but explicitly connected to the protective metabolite phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes naringenin chalcone to naringenin in the early flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms central flavonoid intermediate en route to anthocyanins/flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UV-B-induced CHI expression is reduced in phyB mutants, supporting phyB-dependent flavonoid-mediated UV-B protection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: UV-B inducible in seedlings at 28°C; induction reduced in phyB-9
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in wild type and phyB-9 under UV-B/high temperature
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native gene; no promoter manipulation reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI transcript is induced by UV-B during warm-temperature acclimation. 2. phyB is required for full CHI induction. 3. CHI induction is part of the phyB-dependent protective flavonoid program.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be co-targeted with upstream signaling genes to strengthen UV-B acclimation through flavonoid production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is one of the explicit flavonoid biosynthetic genes assayed for the terminal metabolite phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols into flavonols in the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Generates protective flavonoid products contributing to UV-B tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UV-B induction of FLS1 is reduced in phyB mutants, indicating dependence of protective flavonoid metabolism on phyB
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: UV-B inducible in seedlings at 28°C; reduced in phyB-9
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under UV-B/high temperature in wild type and phyB-9
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native gene; no promoter manipulation reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS1 is induced by UV-B during acclimation at high temperature. 2. phyB mutation attenuates FLS1 induction. 3. This supports a role for active phyB in promoting protective flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to enhance UV-screening flavonoid branch products in engineering or breeding programs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes general flavonoid biosynthesis and anthocyanin accumulation; FLS1 was used as a protective metabolism marker.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UVR8 UV-B photoreceptor with RCC1-like repeats
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for UV-B-triggered suppression of thermomorphogenesis and for downstream protective flavonoid gene activation through COP1 inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed in seedlings under UV-B and high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: UV-B-activated inhibition/sequestration of COP1, leading to stabilization of phyB and HFR1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis and UV-B acclimation indirectly via signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: COP1; indirectly phyB, HFR1, HY5, CHI, CHS, FLS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates protective flavonoid pathway genes indirectly by inhibiting COP1 under UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with COP1 upon UV-B activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: uvr8-6 mutant analysis; hypocotyl assays; phyB immunoblot; qRT-PCR of UV-B-responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential UV-B sensor initiating the signaling cascade that enables phyB-dependent flavonoid accumulation and UV-B tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UVR8 is required for UV-B-mediated suppression of thermomorphogenesis. 2. UV-B stabilization of phyB depends on UVR8. 3. UVR8 acts through COP1 to trigger protective UV-B responses, including flavonoid gene induction.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering stronger or temperature-resilient UVR8-COP1 signaling may improve UV-B acclimation in warm environments
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a TF, UVR8 is treated as a regulatory gene because it is the upstream decisive signaling regulator in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV-B protective metabolite; PIF4 indirectly reduces UV-B tolerance through promoting growth instead of protection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High PIF4 activity reduces UV-B tolerance at high temperature by promoting auxin-mediated thermomorphogenic growth; suppression of PIF4 is required for optimal UV-B acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced at high temperature; transcript partially reduced by UV-B/certain signaling contexts
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein and/or activity strongly regulated in seedlings by COP1, phyB, HFR1, CRY1, UV-B, and temperature
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of growth genes; activity inhibited by phyB/HFR1/CRY1; protein stability reduced under UV-B partly via phyB
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis/signaling-linked thermomorphogenesis that antagonizes UV-B acclimation; indirect effect on flavonoid-associated tolerance phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: High temperature; COP1; phyB; HFR1; CRY1; UVR8-mediated UV-B signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates auxin biosynthesis/signaling and thermomorphogenesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression (35S:PIF4-MYC); endogenous protein immunoblot; mutant combinations including pif4 pif5; qRT-PCR of target genes; UV-B survival assay; PEO-IAA treatment; axr2-1 genetic suppression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator of UV-B tolerance in this study because its thermomorphogenic program compromises acclimation despite not directly lowering flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COP1 promotes PIF4 abundance and activity, while phyB/HFR1/CRY1 suppress it. 2. UV-B reduces PIF4 protein abundance partly through phyB and suppresses its target genes. 3. Elevated PIF4 activity decreases UV-B tolerance, and this defect is rescued by blocking auxin perception/signaling or by pif mutations.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotes elongation growth but compromises UV-B tolerance under high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial attenuation of PIF4 or its auxin output may improve UV-B resilience in hot environments without necessarily reducing flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper frames PIF4 as the central growth regulator downstream of UVR8-COP1-phyB/HFR1; metabolic relevance is indirect via tradeoff between growth and UV-B-protective metabolism/tolerance.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports UV-B induction of flavonoid biosynthetic and protective genes downstream of phyB/UVR8-COP1 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein level elevated by UV-B at 28°C, but less so in phyB-9
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis and UV-B protective gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: HY5 immunoblot in wild type and phyB-9; qRT-PCR of downstream genes; mutant contextual analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator linking phyB accumulation to induction of protective flavonoid biosynthesis under UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UV-B elevates HY5 protein at high temperature. 2. This increase is reduced in phyB-9 mutants. 3. Lower HY5 likely contributes to weaker induction of flavonoid biosynthetic genes and reduced anthocyanin accumulation in phyB mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhancing HY5 stability in warm UV-B environments may strengthen protective flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HY5 is not the main mechanistic focus but is experimentally linked to the metabolite phenotype and thus included as a core regulatory gene.
